frameworks.name: data-driven controllability map
frameworks.one_line: learn finite-horizon reachable manifold from input-output stimulation data without explicit dynamics model

frameworks.name: neural population manifold
frameworks.one_line: stimulation-evoked trajectories traverse a reachable manifold partially overlapping intrinsic activity manifold

frameworks.name: linear-superposition encoding model
frameworks.one_line: responses approximated as linear sum of localized stimulation fields with weak history dependence

frameworks.name: model-based closed-loop neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: approaches requiring detailed in vivo circuit models or trial-and-error open-loop tuning

core_question: Can patterned multi-electrode microstimulation steer cortical population activity to designated target states without an explicit circuit model?
key_assumptions: controllability is learnable from short random pulse-sequence training epochs
weak-stimulation regime yields approximately linear superposition of stimulation fields
population states occupy a low-dimensional manifold accessible via electrode subsets
manipulations: multi-electrode pulse sequences delivered through subsets of electrodes
designated target population states for closed-loop steering
key_findings: REACH-Ctrl achieves high real-time control accuracy in macaque prefrontal cortex
control is robust across sessions and stimulation parameters
reachable manifold shows substantial but incomplete overlap with intrinsic activity manifold
multi-electrode responses are well approximated by linear sum of stimulation fields with modest history dependence
interpretations: population control is feasible without explicit knowledge of connectivity or dynamics
weak patterned microstimulation engages a structured, partially intrinsic reachable subspace
limitations_noted: reachable manifold only partially overlaps the intrinsic activity manifold
linear-superposition approximation holds in the weak-stimulation regime
